metadata.title: Quadrupole Magnetic Field
metadata.loc_class: QC - Physics: plasma and electromagnetic fields
metadata.creator: Daniel Alexander Trawin

master_row.title: Quadrupole Magnetic Field
master_row.canonical_statement: \begin{theorem}[Emergent Quadrupole Topology] For opposing dipoles $\vec{\mu}_1 = -\vec{\mu}_2 = \mu\hat{z}$ separated by constraint vector $\vec{d}$, the composite field exhibits spontaneous quadrupole symmetry breaking: \begin{equation} \mathcal{B}_{\text{quadrupole}}(\vec{r}) = \lim_{\epsilon \to 0^+} \left[\mathcal{B}_{\text{dipole}}^{(1)}(\vec{r}) + \mathcal{B}_{\text{dipole}}^{(2)}(\vec{r}) + \mathcal{O}(\epsilon^{-4})\right] \end{equation} where the singular term enforces topological pocket formation. \end{theorem}
master_row.formation_note: Recovered from a D:\All source document under ‘Quadrupole Pocket Formation via Symmetry Breaking’.
master_row.dictionary: Quadrupole Magnetic Field — B_quadrupole(r) = lim_ε → 0^+ [B_dipole^(1)(r) + B_dipole^(2)(r) + O(ε^-4) ]
master_row.encyclopedia: The entry preserves the equation exactly as extracted from `0_Incoming\Manual_Uploads\TXT_20260224_182616\21.txt` for registry alignment and later derivation.
